title: LakeForcing-OpenDrift: an open, reproducible pipeline for generating hydrodynamic and wind-wave forcing of inland lakes to drive Lagrangian transport models
description: Open Python pipeline that assembles bathymetry (HydroLAKES/GLOBathy/DAHITI) and ERA5 meteorology, auto-configures and runs a coupled Delft3D-FLOW + Delft3D-WAVE simulation for any inland lake, and exports CF-compliant NetCDF that drives the OpenDrift particle tracker. Core contribution: a sigma-layer to z-level coupling (currents, temperature, water level and wave-driven Stokes drift). Demonstrated unchanged on 12 lakes on all inhabited continents (36S-60N); benchmarked against an expert-built reservoir model and against independent satellite lake-surface-temperature observations. The sigma-to-z exporter is covered by a continuous-integration test on a small bundled Delft3D fixture, so it runs with no Delft3D installation.

notes: This Zenodo record archives the LakeForcing-OpenDrift source code (MIT). The generated twelve-lake forcing dataset (CC-BY-4.0) and full reproducibility data are distributed as release assets at https://github.com/vaspapa79/LakeForcing-OpenDrift/releases.
